duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Grantor releaseRate miscalculation in transferVesting: divides by numOfSteps instead of (numOfSteps - stepsClaimed), inflating per-step claim rate and enabling token theft from vesting contract
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same math error at SecondSwap_StepVesting.transferVesting line ~153-154 where releaseRate = totalAmount/numOfSteps instead of (totalAmount-amountClaimed)/(numOfSteps-stepsClaimed). TECH-H011 describes the compound insolvency impact and ECON-H013 describes the downstream available() underflow, but these are consequences of the identical root cause. ECON-H001 is most comprehensive with attack economics, victim impact, and clear exploit scenario.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: VestingManager acts as single intermediary beneficiary, pooling stepsClaimed from first seller and contaminating all subsequent buyer vesting schedules
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same architectural flaw: VestingManager holds a single _vestings record per plan, first seller's stepsClaimed becomes permanent, and all buyers inherit it regardless of actual seller. Same root cause in _createVesting for existing beneficiary not updating stepsClaimed. ECON-H002 selected for most detailed attack economics and strategic exploitation scenario.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Referral fees calculated in _handleTransfers but never transferred to referrer; feeCollector receives the full buyerFeeTotal + sellerFeeTotal
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the identical missing transfer: referralFeeCost is computed but no safeTransfer to _referral exists, and feeCollectorTotal = buyerFeeTotal + sellerFeeTotal without deduction. Same function (_handleTransfers), same bug (missing transfer line). Note: TECH2-H004 ID appears twice in input data — the referral instance is removed here. ECON-H003 chosen for comprehensive impact analysis.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Marketplace freeze blocks unlistVesting via isFreeze modifier, preventing all callers including admin from recovering listed vesting tokens during freeze
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same design flaw: isFreeze modifier on unlistVesting runs before admin authorization check, blocking emergency unlisting during freeze. Same function, same modifier, same impact (locked vesting tokens). TECH-H004 selected for clearest technical explanation of modifier ordering issue.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: No upper bound validation on discountPct in listVesting allows values >= BASE (10000), causing underflow revert in _getDiscountedPrice and creating permanently unpurchasable listings
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same missing input validation: discountPct not bounded below BASE in listVesting, causing (BASE - discountPct) underflow in _getDiscountedPrice. Same two functions, same arithmetic issue. TECH-H005 selected for most precise proof_of_signal with specific line references for both listing and price calculation.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Division by zero in _createVesting when transferring to new beneficiary after all vesting steps elapsed (numOfSteps == stepsClaimed)
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same division by zero: releaseRate = _totalAmount / (numOfSteps - _stepsClaimed) when _stepsClaimed == numOfSteps in _createVesting for new beneficiaries. ECON2-H010 specifically focuses on VestingManager as new beneficiary but same root cause. ECON-H009 selected for covering both direct transfer and marketplace purchase scenarios with impact analysis.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: PARTIAL listing type does not validate minPurchaseAmt against listing amount, allowing minPurchaseAmt > amount which creates degenerate listings
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same missing validation: the require statement only checks minPurchaseAmt bounds for SINGLE listings, leaving PARTIAL listings unvalidated. Same function (listVesting), same validation gap, same impact. TECH-H007 selected for most detailed analysis including _validatePurchase interaction.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Checks-effects-interactions violation in spotPurchase: _handleTransfers executes external token transfers before listing.balance is decremented, enabling reentrancy via callback-enabled currency tokens
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same CEI violation: external safeTransferFrom/safeTransfer calls in _handleTransfers occur before listing.balance -= _amount in spotPurchase. Same two functions, same state update ordering bug, same reentrancy vector. ECON-H006 selected for most comprehensive analysis including addCoin validation gap and detailed attack economics.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: penaltyFee initialized to 10 ether (10*10^18) in MarketplaceSetting constructor, equating to ~10 trillion USDT with 6-decimal tokens, making early unlisting impossible
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same configuration defect: penaltyFee = 10 ether in constructor with 6-decimal USDT creates unpayable penalty. TECH-H017 adds the setPenaltyFee bounds angle but same root issue. TECH-H009 selected for most detailed analysis including deployment script evidence and chain-specific context.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: VestingManager.setMaxSellPercent accepts any uint256 without upper bound validation, allowing token issuer to set values exceeding BASE and bypass sell restrictions
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same missing validation: no require(maxSellPercent <= BASE) in setMaxSellPercent. Same function, same parameter, same impact of enabling unbounded selling. TECH-H010 selected for most precise proof_of_signal.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: whitelistAddress() in SecondSwap_Whitelist lacks access control, allowing any user to self-whitelist and bypass private sale restrictions
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same access control failure: whitelistAddress() is external with no caller restriction, enabling self-whitelisting. Same function, same missing modifier, same bypass of private sale gate. Note: TECH2-H003 ID appears twice in input — the whitelist instance is removed here. ECON-H004 selected for most comprehensive economic analysis with attack cost/profit assessment.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Upgradeable implementation contracts (Marketplace, VestingManager, VestingDeployer) lack _disableInitializers() in constructors, allowing direct initialization of implementation contracts
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same missing best practice: no constructor calling _disableInitializers() in Initializable contracts. Same three contracts, same vulnerability pattern. OPS-H011 selected for most detailed analysis with deployment evidence from TypeScript deployment scripts.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: doesFunctionExist uses staticcall which returns true for contracts with fallback functions, providing unreliable interface detection that can cause incorrect decimals/pricing or DoS
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same unreliable detection mechanism: staticcall returns success for contracts with fallback functions regardless of whether decimals() exists. Same function (doesFunctionExist), same false-positive pattern, same downstream impact on pricing math. Note: TECH2-H004 ID appears twice in input — the doesFunctionExist/griefing instance is removed here. TECH-H016 selected for most comprehensive technical analysis covering both false positive and pricing corruption.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: WhitelistDeployer.deployWhitelist() is permissionless, allowing any actor to deploy whitelist contracts and emit WhitelistCreated events that may confuse off-chain systems
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the same permissionless factory function with no access control on deployWhitelist(). Same contract, same function, same spoofing risk. TECH-H013 selected for more detailed analysis.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Deploying StepVesting with numOfSteps > (endTime - startTime) causes stepDuration to truncate to 0, permanently bricking claims via division by zero
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the same deployment validation gap: stepDuration = (endTime - startTime) / numOfSteps truncates to 0 when steps exceed duration, causing claimable() to divide by zero. ECON2-H009 selected for more detailed analysis with attack economics and explicit proof_of_signal lines.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Unbounded array in createVestings enables gas-limit DoS when processing large beneficiary batches
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the same unbounded loop in createVestings over _beneficiaries array with safeTransferFrom per iteration. Same function, same gas exhaustion risk. Note: TECH2-H003 ID is duplicated in input — the batch vesting instance is removed here. ECON-H019 selected as representative.
